problem: PowerShell 5.1 rejects && and || pipeline chain operators with a parser error
solution: Use 'A; if ($?) { B }' to run B only if A succeeds, or 'A; B' to chain unconditionally. Ternary and ?? are also unavailable in 5.1 - use if/else.

problem: Claude Code PreToolUse hook needs to block a tool call and explain why
solution: Exit code 2 from the hook blocks the call; whatever the hook wrote to stderr is fed back to Claude as the explanation. Exit 0 allows. Hook receives tool_name/tool_input as JSON on stdin.

problem: python http.server serves cached/stale files when a dashboard polls JSON state
solution: Subclass SimpleHTTPRequestHandler and send Cache-Control: no-store in end_headers(); also fetch with {cache:'no-store'} client-side. Then polling always sees fresh state.

problem: bash heredoc/echo mangles backslashes in JSON test payloads on Windows git bash
solution: Never pipe hand-quoted JSON with backslash paths through bash on Windows - escapes collapse and json.load fails silently. Build payloads in Python with json.dumps and subprocess.run(input=...), like .claude/hooks/test_guard.py does.

problem: Claude Code hook shows no spawn event when conductor hires a subagent
solution: The subagent tool is named Agent in Claude Code 2.x (Task in older builds), and PostToolUse fires only at completion. Emit the spawn from PreToolUse and match tool_name in ('Task','Agent') - see .claude/hooks/mirror.py

problem: git bash on Windows rewrites CLI args that start with a slash into C:/Program Files/Git/... paths
solution: MSYS path conversion mangles arguments like /webdesign. Pass them double-slashed (//webdesign), set MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1, or do the write in Python instead of the shell. Sibling trap to the backslash-JSON one.

problem: focus a specific spawned Claude Code terminal window from a background server on Windows
solution: Launch each instance via conhost.exe (cmd /k ... claude) so the conhost process OWNS the window; store that pid; to focus, EnumWindows + GetWindowThreadProcessId==pid then SwitchToThisWindow. Title-matching fails because Claude rewrites the console title after launch. See dashboard/serve.py focus_by().

problem: dashboard layout blows out horizontally on user's display and 'nothing shows up'
solution: Grid/flex children default to min-width:auto, so any nowrap text (mono event strings) forces the track wider than the viewport and pushes columns off-screen. Fix: .grid>*{min-width:0} on every grid/flex child that holds text, overflow-x:clip on main, and ALWAYS verify at ~1280px CSS viewport (Daniel's effective width at his display scaling), not just 1600.

problem: claude -p critic in an agent loop returns empty result / error_max_turns
solution: A -p one-shot judge WILL try tools and blow --max-turns 1, returning subtype error_max_turns with NO result field. Fix: --disallowedTools * plus max-turns 2 and an explicit do-not-use-tools line in the prompt. Treat worker error_max_turns as a partial turn (resume next round), not a crash.

problem: ssh host 'command' says claude command not found though interactive login works
solution: ssh with a command runs a NON-login shell: ~/.profile and nvm never load, so anything in ~/.local/bin or npm/nvm bin is missing from PATH. Prefix the remote command with sourcing /etc/profile ~/.profile ~/.bashrc nvm.sh and export PATH=~/.local/bin:...:PATH. No double quotes if it rides inside cmd.exe quotes.

problem: store an ssh password safely on Windows with zero dependencies and feed it to ssh automatically
solution: DPAPI via ctypes crypt32 CryptProtectData/CryptUnprotectData (user scope - only this Windows account decrypts), base64 in a gitignored json. Feed to ssh with SSH_ASKPASS_REQUIRE=force + SSH_ASKPASS=.cmd shim that runs a python helper; helper answers yes to host-key prompts and prints the password otherwise. Degrades gracefully to typing in the window.

problem: dashboard instance cards and wire sessions are two unlinked lists for the same thing
solution: Pass MAESTRO_SID=<sid> in the spawned process env; hook scripts inherit env, so mirror.py prefers os.environ MAESTRO_SID over the hook session_id. Wire events then land under the dashboard's own instance id and cards can show the live stage rail/agents/last action. One env var unifies instances, sessions, and orchestrations.

problem: force-directed graph freezes dead on hover
solution: Don't gate physics() on hover - run it every frame with a speed factor (drag 1, hover ~0.22, idle 1) applied to the position integration step, and drop the random jiggle only while hovering. Add neighbor highlight from a prebuilt adjacency: fade non-neighbors to 0.16, tint connected edges, with a .18s css opacity transition.

problem: expanded/selected UI state flashes back on a polling dashboard
solution: Any innerHTML re-render wipes DOM state. Key each expandable element (data-k) and keep a Set of open keys in JS; render reapplies the open class from the Set. Same pattern for any toggle that must survive a poll loop.

problem: show Claude Code plan usage and time to the 5h reset on a local dashboard
solution: No public usage API for subscription accounts - approximate locally: scan ~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl with mtime inside the window, sum message.usage tokens for entries whose timestamp is within the last 5h; window start = earliest such timestamp, reset = start+5h. Limits are per-plan and unpublished, so keep them as user config, not hardcoded.

problem: track a Claude account 5h/7d usage + reset accurately when accounts are swapped in one terminal
solution: Transcripts under a config dir do NOT record which account sent each message, so scanning them mis-attributes usage after a /login swap. Instead read the true server-side window: capture each account OAuth token from <config_dir>/.credentials.json claudeAiOauth.accessToken (email/uuid from <dir>/.claude.json oauthAccount), send a 1-token POST /v1/messages with Authorization: Bearer <token> + header anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20, and read response headers anthropic-ratelimit-unified-5h-{reset,utilization} and -7d-. count_tokens returns NO ratelimit headers; a 429 (window exhausted) STILL carries them. Cache/throttle ~180s. Store swapped-through accounts in a gitignored seen-cache keyed by accountUuid so a one-terminal workflow accumulates all accounts.

problem: python http.server dashboard: new routes 404 intermittently after code changes
solution: Stale server processes accumulate bound to the same port (SocketServer allow_reuse_address lets a second bind succeed); requests round-robin across old+new listeners so newly added routes 404 randomly. Kill ALL pids on the port before restarting: netstat -ano | grep 127.0.0.1:PORT.*LISTEN -> taskkill /F each unique pid, then start exactly one.

problem: carousel arrow buttons do not scroll a CSS scroll-snap container
solution: element.scrollTo({left,behavior:smooth}) is a no-op / overshoots to the end on an overflow-x:auto element with scroll-snap-type set. Fix: animate scrollLeft yourself with a requestAnimationFrame easeOutCubic tween while temporarily setting style.scrollSnapType=none, then restore it so drag-snapping still works. Also: a 2.5s poll that rewrites the track innerHTML resets scrollLeft and detaches buttons mid-click - guard the rebuild behind a content-signature (only re-render when the HTML actually changed).

problem: SSH from the dashboard fails: 'X is not recognized' x3 then Permission denied (publickey,password)
solution: Win32-OpenSSH runs the SSH_ASKPASS helper WITHOUT quoting its path, so a space in the path (repo under 'Python Env') makes CreateProcess treat everything before the space as the exe -> 'is not recognized', askpass returns no password, ssh denies. Fix: set SSH_ASKPASS to the Windows 8.3 SHORT path via GetShortPathNameW (PYTHON~1, no spaces); fall back to the full path if 8.3 names are disabled. cwd=ROOT and the remote command are unaffected (CreateProcess handles spaces there).

problem: soul directory md file count
solution: Found 2 .md files in soul/ directory: CHANGELOG.md, soul.md

problem: Sonnet structured output (output_config json_schema) intermittently returns malformed/truncated JSON
solution: Adaptive thinking is ON by default on Sonnet 5 and its thinking tokens COUNT AGAINST max_tokens; a 1200 cap left ~200 tokens for the JSON and truncated it mid-brief. Fix: raise max_tokens (3500), slice text[find('{'):rfind('}')+1] before json.loads, and report stop_reason==max_tokens as a distinct error.

problem: orchestrator auto account routing picked an account that immediately failed with weekly limit hit
solution: Claude accounts have TWO unified windows (5h and 7d). Routing on 5h utilization alone picks accounts at 0%/5h but 100%/7d which reject work. least_used must score max(pct5h, pct7d) from the anthropic-ratelimit-unified-* headers.

problem: Seek bar jumps backwards during playback due to stale server progress timestamps
solution: Use local ticker to advance playback progress between polls; resync seek bar to server value only on actual track/pause state changes, not on every poll. This prevents snapping backwards when stale progress_ms values arrive from the server.

problem: Brain learning not capturing solutions — read path exists but write path is manual
solution: Register a Stop hook in ~/.claude/settings.json that runs after each turn: reads transcript, uses Haiku to judge if learning is durable (root causes/gotchas/recipes, not chatter), dedupes against Hermes (≥0.75 similarity), writes to solved.jsonl + mirrors to vault + emits learn event. Closes the write loop so every session auto-feeds the brain.

problem: Dashboard revamp needed more motion and visual density without AI slop
solution: Kept the vanilla dashboard and royal plum/paper theme, added a visible desktop task rail, live ops visual stage, mission stack ring, route panning, scroll-trigger reveal, pointer tilt card motion, and morphing active-run panel. Verified with Playwright desktop/mobile screenshots and a console-clean smoke check.

problem: Three.js plane animation jerky; mid-flight keyframe stall and broken quaternion interpolation freezing frames
solution: Replace segmented flight path with one continuous eased animation curve; replace broken `slerpQuaternions` call with three.js built-in `Quaternion.slerp()`; reduce damping coefficient (k=3.4) for smoother interpolation; ensure quaternion is travel-aligned and wings-level throughout

problem: Unitree G1 sim2real dance tracking: policy trained with ≤20ms latency randomization fell after 44s on hardware due to actual sensorimotor latency of 40–80ms (measured 4 independent ways)
solution: Root cause is not language/comms overhead (0.16ms) but actuator response + leg-odometry lag inside robot. Fix: retrain with 0–80ms latency domain randomization + root-pos weight 1.0. Python SDK sufficient; C++ would shave only 1–2ms jitter. Domain randomization robustness beats low-level optimization for this constraint.

problem: Real-time robot control pipeline had undetected jitter in motion retargeting causing visible twitching during fast hand/body coordination moves in live shows
solution: Added frame-level temporal smoothing + outlier rejection before velocity clamp in retargeting pipeline, plus jerk metric in verification gate to auto-flag glitchy motions before robot execution. Measure baseline spike stats first, then validate smoothing filter effectiveness over 5-min safe telemetry run before deployment.

problem: Unitree G1 robot deployment safety on Windows: POSIX signal handling for damping on exit is unverified; bash runbooks incompatible
solution: Deploy actual robot commands only from Ubuntu 22.04 (use original laptop with validated tv env + .secrets/, or dual-boot/WSL2). Windows safe for code/training/prep only. Network: wired Ethernet, static IP 192.168.123.2/255.255.255.0. Python env: unitree_sdk2_python + onnxruntime. First contact: read-only mode (--mode read) to validate SDK/network with zero motion risk.

problem: Motion twitching caused by isolated single-frame jerk spikes (5-15× baseline) in all dance CSVs
solution: Apply robust z-score outlier rejection (per-joint MAD, z>10, floor 150 rad/s²) with cubic-spline re-interpolation, then Savitzky-Golay smoothing (window 7, polyorder 3) in joint space and quaternion tangent-space (slerp-aware) for root orientation. Wire before velocity clamp in prep_motion pipeline. Verified: spike frames reduced 96-100%, jerk peaks cut 4-20×, sharp choreography preserved (≤0.04 rad RMS fidelity loss).

problem: Preview videos missing from frontend; .gitignore excludes media files (*.mp4, *.npz, data/policies/*) so they exist only on source machine
solution: Copy data/previews/, data/policies/, data/jobs/ directories from original machine via USB/scp, or regenerate previews locally from tracked CSV files using repo's MuJoCo renderer. Gitignored media won't clone automatically.

problem: Playwright tests polluted production data by recording fake incidents against live records, silently corrupting their state (demoting show-ready to sim-verified, zeroing clean streaks)
solution: Point Playwright tests at throwaway/sandbox records only, never against live library. Audit test setup to isolate test incidents from production state before pushing.

problem: Double-counting mechanical lag in sim2real fidelity retrain kills station-keeping
solution: Cap curriculum latency at 60 ms (not 80) when sim PD loop already models mechanical lag. Three-stage resume: 0–20 ms (4k iter), 0–50 ms (+3k), 0–60 ms (+3k). Verify at 40 ms+push AND drift <1 m.

problem: Exam signing key (32 raw bytes) failed validation after text round-trip extraction due to appended newline
solution: Strip trailing newline from extracted signing key before use; verify against a committed signed verdict to catch byte-exactness failures early. Store as raw bytes, not text.

problem: __webpack_modules__[moduleId] is not a function runtime error in Next.js dev server
solution: Corrupted .next cache from running `next build` while dev server was active. Delete the .next folder and restart `npm run dev` to regenerate. Rule: never run `next build` concurrently with dev server.

problem: Next.js dev server returns 500 Internal Server Error on all routes after .next cache deleted
solution: Stop the running dev server (Ctrl+C) and restart with `npm run dev`. The live process cannot survive its cache being removed; it must be restarted to rebuild. First page load takes ~15s while recompiling.

problem: OpenSky API calls fail silently after OAuth2 migration despite valid credentials in credentials.json
solution: Environment variables OPENSKY_CLIENT_ID and OPENSKY_CLIENT_SECRET must be set in .env files (aeolus/.env for Docker, aeolus/apps/web/.env.local for Next dev relay), not read from credentials.json. OpenSky now blocks anonymous/unauthenticated requests. Restart dev server after creating .env files.

problem: Skill tree UI with horizontal scrolling is hard to navigate; needs clearer hierarchy, dark mode, and concise descriptions
solution: Rebuild as vertical spine layout (genres stack top-to-bottom, skills grid-wrap within sections). Add dark-mode toggle in topbar that persists via localStorage (applied before first paint to avoid white flash). Cap skill descriptions to 1–2 sentences in detail panel, with metadata as chips. Keep account cards white in dark mode for brand consistency while re-scoping text colors locally for readability.

problem: UI maximalism: cursor-tracking 3D card tilt + decorative rings on every card create visual noise; sparse cards stretch to equal height leaving empty voids
solution: Remove perspective transform handlers (rotateX/rotateY on pointer-move); replace with scroll entrance + hover lift only. Strip concentric rings from account cards (keep one dark accent card as focal point, not repetition). Set carousel to align-items:flex-start so cards size to content, eliminating voids.

problem: Brain/knowledge base filling with routine noise instead of signal: every completed mission auto-logged, every role told to record, cheap first-try successes polluted the vault
solution: Add `_worth_remembering()` gate that writes to brain only on: failure | cost ≥$0.15 | ≥2 roles | opus/fable reasoning | ≥40 turns depth. Named constants (`WORTH_COST`, `WORTH_TURNS`) allow one-line weight tuning. Apply gate in CEO pipeline (mission-level) and orchestrator accept path. Prune existing noise from vault + jsonl, regenerate index.

problem: Split-button dropdown menu with model/effort/stats overrides was stripped from Run button during UI revamp
solution: Restore split-button + popover pattern: (1) add override-handling backend in plan_and_start(text, opts) to apply tuning after CEO plans; (2) re-insert CSS for split-button + popover UI; (3) wire JS to pass opts object on send and populate Account list live from configured accounts; (4) add visual indicator (white dot on chevron) when non-Auto overrides are active

problem: Model client must keep caller loops alive when a model refuses
solution: Fable 5 (and Opus/Sonnet) return safety refusals as HTTP 200 with stop_reason='refusal', NOT an error. In model_client.normalize(), collapse every outcome (refusal, HTTP error, truncation, clean answer) into one 200-shaped envelope with a stopped_reason field: None on clean end_turn, else the reason ('refusal'/'http_500'/'max_tokens'). Callers branch on stopped_reason and re-route; they never raise/exit. Also: Fable thinking is always-on so omit the thinking param and control depth via output_config.effort; xhigh is gated behind an agentic-loop flag (else -> high); model swap to Opus is one constant (MODEL=MODEL_IDS['opus']).

problem: Anthropic Fable 5 returns refusals as HTTP 200 with stop_reason='refusal', not as HTTP 4xx errors
solution: Intercept refusals by checking `stop_reason == 'refusal'` in the HTTP 200 response body, not by catching exceptions. Normalize all outcomes (refusal, error, truncation, success) into a single 200 envelope with `stopped_reason` field for consistent downstream handling.

problem: Cron daily-review loop must be validatable offline and survive a model refusal mid-sweep
solution: loop.sh (cron entry): absolute ROOT via BASH_SOURCE, minimal PATH, load .env with set -a, atomic run-lock via mkdir (portable, no flock), once-per-day .last-run stamp for idempotency; --dry-run bypasses both guards. review.py: scan=haiku(cheap)->findings shaped as the task contract; escalate=opus writes A-Z plan, model tag reused from model_client._DELEGATE by complexity. Refusal handling keys on env['stopped_reason'] from normalize(); dry-run injects a refusal by building normalize({stop_reason:'refusal'}) on the 2nd finding so the continue-not-exit path is provable with no API key.

problem: Model refusals (HTTP 200 + stopped_reason field) must be intercepted mid-loop without hard-exiting the entire pipeline
solution: Key on `env['stopped_reason']` from model_client.normalize() after every call. Mark finding 'blocked', log the refusal reason, continue to next item. Refusals are HTTP 200 with stop_reason field, not HTTP errors—normalize() collapses all outcomes (refusal/error/truncation/success) into one 200 envelope.

problem: agent-card status is a whole-record emit: a transition after push_feedback wiped the note
solution: set_card writes the FULL card each call, so flipping a role to 'working' right after pushing a feedback note re-emits with note='' and silently erases it. Carry forward fields you must preserve: load_card first, re-pass note=c['note']. Applies to any field not in the transition (cost, stopped_reason too).

problem: I want you to orchestrate and revamp this system(u will use fable 5) to make it even more automatic (and make it way easier manage and view everything on my end) add a system that
solution: architect=done; review-loop=done; orchestration=done; grading-audit=failed; health-ui=blocked; integrator=blocked. You've hit your session limit · resets 6:50pm (Asia/Bangkok)

problem: Multi-step missions fail at one role, blocking downstream roles with no way to recover without losing prior completed work
solution: Implement resume capability: detect failed/blocked roles, keep completed role outputs in context, reset failed+blocked roles to pending, re-run from failure point. Guard against empty resume queue. Surface in UI as recoverable 'LIMIT' badge + 'Continue where it left off' button with hint showing which roles restart.

problem: Auto-archive logic stamps `updated` timestamp on every `_save()`, breaking age-based archive tests because finished missions get re-saved and appear fresh
solution: For testing auto-archive thresholds, write test files directly to disk bypassing `_save()` to preserve their true age. In production `updated = now` on finish is correct behavior (a mission's last save *is* its completion time).

problem: Smoothing a cumulative activity curve (monotonic data) in the dashboard graph
solution: Use monotone-cubic (Fritsch-Carlson) interpolation, not Catmull-Rom. Catmull-Rom overshoots/undershoots on flat-then-steep stretches and draws a false backward dip in a curve that must only ever rise. Monotone cubic clamps tangents (alpha^2+beta^2>9 => scale by 3/sqrt(s)) so the spline is guaranteed non-decreasing. See dashboard/index.html monoPath().

problem: Catmull-Rom spline interpolation causes false backward dips on cumulative monotonic data (flat→steep transitions), breaking visual integrity of cumulative activity graphs
solution: Use Fritsch–Carlson monotone-cubic spline instead of Catmull-Rom. Fritsch–Carlson enforces monotonicity—if input is monotonically increasing, output curve never dips backward. Verified: 0-pixel backward deviation on 492 live audit events.

problem: Wiring the six components end-to-end: is loop.sh a live scan->plan->delegate->grade->execute pipeline?
solution: No, and by design. loop.sh runs review.py (live scan->manager plan, refusal-surviving) then skilltree.py (all six component CONTRACTS exercised offline in a temp dir). The review->orchestration->grading->execute handoff is NOT auto-chained: review.py writes plans to state/review/<date>.json; delegation/grading/execution are operator-gated via the PENDING-CONFIRMATION gate (orchestrate.execute re-reads persisted status) and driven from the dashboard/ceo.py, never by cron. The 'one cohesive run covering all six' is the skilltree.py health check, not a production chain. Component 6 (UI graph) is a read-only view of state/events.jsonl (renderPerf), verified live in a browser, not by the offline check. Minimal fix applied: added the PENDING-CONFIRMATION gate assertion to skilltree.check_orchestration so the single dry-run command actually proves 'proposals never auto-execute'.

problem: loop.sh does not auto-chain review→orchestration→grading→execute; review.py writes plans to state/review/<date>.json but nothing consumes them for auto-staffing/execution in the cron path
solution: By design: review.py (scan→plan) is live; delegation/grading/execution are operator-gated via PENDING-CONFIRMATION and driven from dashboard. loop.sh --dry-run is a health check proving all six contracts hold, not a live auto-execute pipeline. Do not wire auto-execution at 07:00; the gate exists for safety.

problem: desktop.py launcher fails silently when stale server processes or browser profile locks remain from prior start/stop churn, producing blank/no window despite app being healthy
solution: Rewrite launcher to: (1) detect & reuse healthy server on port 8817, kill stale listeners before fresh start, (2) catch server startup errors and print them, (3) suppress browser first-run prompts and fall back to default browser if Edge hands off to existing instance instead of spawning window

problem: I want you to orchestrate and revamp this system(u will use fable 5) to make it even more automatic (and make it way easier manage and view everything on my end) add a system that
solution:
architect=done; review-loop=done; orchestration=done; grading-audit=done; health-ui=done; integrator=done. Everything verifies. Nothing to build or patch — prior roles shipped it; my job as conductor was to confirm the wiring holds end-to-end, and it does.

## Verification results

**Single command — `bash loop.sh --dry-run` — all checks pass (offline, no API key, no writes to the live wire):**

|

problem: Dashboard shows stale UI after CEO mission rewrites the page while a window is open—changes are live on disk and served, but the client never reloads its HTML/CSS/JS shell, only re-fetches data every
solution: Add /api/version endpoint returning UI mtime; client polls it and shows 'Dashboard was updated—click to reload' banner when mtime changes. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) or restart desktop.py immediately shows changes.

problem: Every prompt spins up full CEO orchestration with Opus roles even for simple questions like 'what does X do', wasting tokens and latency
solution: Add Mode control (Auto/Direct/Delegate) to command-bar dropdown. In Auto mode, refiner judges simplicity and routes simple prompts (lookup, explain, summarize) directly to Haiku/Sonnet with zero agents, tagged with `kind` field so frontend branches answer-vs-mission. Complex work still delegates to CEO roster. Commit f8079cd.

problem: Fix the archive button on the dashboard and audit the wire for repeated manual work
solution: archivefix=failed; workflowaudit=failed; consolidate=failed. You've hit your session limit · resets 8:10pm (Asia/Bangkok)

problem: HTTP request hangs 23-180s doing Opus planning inside request, causing 'Failed to fetch' and killing missions on server restart
solution: Make planning async: create mission immediately, run planning on background thread. Mission returns in 'planning' state with no roles yet, fills in as they're decided. Also add local heuristic triage (no API call): only short prompts <180 chars get Haiku rewrite; long detailed prompts go verbatim to CEO.

problem: Chat-only mode mistakenly used for file/command tasks, inventing fake outputs instead of actually running tools
solution: Route non-question prompts (file reads, commands, API calls) to solo Claude Code runs with explicit no-delegate brief; add local heuristic: anything touching filesystem/network is minimum solo, never answer-only

problem: Where is the daily briefing in the morning we hardcoded at 7AM to go through stuff ive done in the day in my git repo/calendar plans, implement it and make sure it works locally wh
solution: solo=done. (no final message — used all 40 turns)

problem: Dashboard 'Daily briefing' card showed 'briefing unavailable - server offline?' even though the server was up and healthy
solution: The daily briefing feature (daily_briefing.py + /api/briefing route in dashboard/serve.py + UI card in index.html) was added while a serve.py process was already running. desktop.py's self-healing check only pings '/' to decide a server is 'healthy' and reuses it rather than restarting -- so the live process kept serving its old in-memory code with no /api/briefing route (404). jget() on the frontend swallows failed optional fetches into '', so S.briefing became null and the UI showed the generic offline fallback. Fix: kill the stale PID listening on the dashboard port and relaunch dashboard/serve.py so it picks up new Python routes; static HTML/JS changes are picked up live but backend .py route changes are not until the process restarts. Any future edit to serve.py, ceo.py, pulse.py, chat.py, orchestrator.py, or daily_briefing.py requires a server restart, not just a page reload.

problem: Briefing card was offline again despite the route existing; port_alive() in desktop.py only pinged '/' so a stale server process (predating a backend .py edit) kept getting reused forever
solution: Added a 'boot' timestamp to /api/version (dashboard/serve.py) and made desktop.py's port_alive() compare it against the mtimes of every backend .py the server imports (serve.py, ceo.py, chat.py, orchestrator.py, pulse.py, askpass.py, daily_briefing.py). If any of those files were edited after the running server's boot time, port_alive() now returns False so main() kills and restarts it automatically -- no more manual PID hunting after backend edits.

problem: Rune agent dies silently mid-task with status 'done' when prompt exhausts turn budget; Continue button restarts instead of resuming
solution: In ceo.py, fix exhaustion handling: change `if w.get("is_error") and not ran_out:` to `if w.get("is_error") and ran_out:`, set role status to 'exhausted' with reason, auto-continue same session ID instead of wiping result. Make UI show reason field. Guard server shutdown to not kill in-flight mission threads.

problem: Microsoft Graph refresh tokens rotate on every use and must be persisted, else calendar silently fails with invalid_grant after ~24h
solution: Write new refresh token back to state/pulse.json immediately after OAuth token response. Check token age/validity before use and prompt '--outlook-login' if expired rather than silently dropping calendar data.

problem: Outlook calendar integration requires Azure app registration with device-code flow authentication
solution: Register app in portal.azure.com → Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations with redirect URI blank and 'Allow public client flows' enabled. Add Microsoft Graph Calendars.Read permission. Paste Application (client) ID into state/pulse.json under outlook.client_id, then run `python dashboard/pulse.py --outlook-login` and approve the device code at microsoft.com/devicelogin. Alternatively skip Azure entirely by using Outlook's published .ics URL at calendar.ics_url or state/calendar.ics (read-only, staler but no registration needed).

problem: Microsoft Graph device-code flow requires admin consent and AADSTS65001 error when tenant hasn't approved
solution: Use Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools public client (no app registration needed). Run `python dashboard/pulse.py --outlook-login`, complete device code flow at login.microsoft.com/device. If AADSTS65001 returned, admin approval is pending—rerun command after approval granted. Requires Calendars.Read and offline_access scopes.

problem: model_client surfaced stopped_reason but never logged it, so a caller ignoring the return still failed silently
solution: Log at the single choke point normalize() (every outcome converges there), not per caller: one log.warning when stopped is set. Covers refusal/http_500/max_tokens/api_error(exceptions) at once. Proof: test_stopped_reason.py

problem: Mission failures can be silently swallowed if caller ignores stopped_reason return value; normalize() surfaces but never logs non-success outcomes
solution: Add logging at the single convergence point in normalize() where all four failure modes (refusal/http_500/max_tokens/exception) collapse to stopped_reason envelope. One log.warning() call covers all paths; no changes to caller loop or exception handling needed.

problem: Uncommitted verification proof and staged deletions blocking merge; unclear which working-tree changes belong to the fix
solution: Before merging a fix branch, explicitly commit all verification artifacts (test files, logs) and intentional deletions to the branch; discard scratch files. Run full test suite on the committed branch state, then merge with clean working tree. This avoids losing verification work or accidentally pulling uncontrolled material into main.

problem: Do the agentic os branch feature but utilize opus model for them Prove missions cannot die silently, then merge the fix branch
solution:
implementbranch=done; verifynosilentfail=done; mergetomain=done. The working tree is dirty, so per the constraints I need to stop here rather than merge.

problem: As a ceo u delegated these task in memory so do this: Persist Aeolus scenario state and make replays deterministic, The README concedes no database or cache is provisioned — the AP
solution: solo=failed. Your organization has disabled Claude subscription access for Claude Code · Use an Anthropic API key instead, or ask your admin to enable access

problem: Aeolus replay/persistence: recovery plans wouldn't reproduce across replays even with a seeded predictor
solution: Root cause was two-fold: (1) SimulationEngine.trigger_event unconditionally re-stamped event['triggered_at'] with datetime.now() even when a replayed event already carried the original timestamp — and CascadePredictor seeds its RNG from sha256(repr(sorted(event.items()))), so every replay silently got a new seed. Fix: only stamp id/triggered_at if not already present. (2) OR-Tools CP-SAT with num_search_workers>1 under a wall-clock time limit is not reproducible run-to-run (worker race), even with the same model/seed — needed a deterministic=True optimizer mode forcing num_search_workers=1 + fixed random_seed for replay, while leaving production's 4-worker config untouched.

problem: ADS-B live feed returning empty results across multiple regions due to three compounding bugs in flights-live route
solution: 1) Reduce radius from 2,500 nm to 1,400 nm (covers CONUS without hemisphere bloat). 2) Increase abort timeout from 6s to 20s (feeds take 6.2s+ for 250nm alone, need fit within Edge's 25s window). 3) Add server-side caching with `Cache-Control: s-maxage=15, stale-while-revalidate=120` to prevent 429 rate-limits from burst client requests hitting adsb.lol directly. Parallel fan-out was measured and rejected due to per-IP rate limiter.

problem: Live ADS-B simulator backend silently ignoring live schedules in recovery routes due to static YAML cache bypass
solution: Modified `_load_network(engine)` to check engine's working schedule first before falling back to static YAML; ensures `/recovery/*` routes compute over actual live planes when `POST /simulator/reseed-live` has been called

problem: MILP solver status badge showing 'OPTIMAL' on all plans carries zero information—every solve is trivial optimal for its own objective—while looking fake/untrustworthy
solution: Render SolverStatus only when abnormal (heuristic fallback, infeasible). Plan identity carried by objective title instead. Status badge now signals actionable information only.

problem: Flight map filtering: which filters are worth building given existing data from /api/flights-live and derivable client-side metrics
solution: Prioritize: (1) Airline multi-select from airline_iata, (2) Flight phase derived from altitude_ft + vertical_fpm thresholds (climbing >+500, cruising ~0, descending <-500 fpm), (3) Disruption state from flightStates. Skip aircraft type (requires t field not in current route) and origin/destination country (data too sparse). Use compact filter strip UI matching legend voice, not dropdowns.

problem: Financial plan cards buried cost explanations behind 'More' expander, forcing operators to click to compare plans
solution: Surface cost ledger directly on card face: composition bar (cancellations/delays/repositioning), line-item breakdown with swatches, delta vs. cheapest plan, and provenance line. Move narrative/operational impact to expander. Delete orphaned CostBar component.

problem: Next.js dev server serving stale components when OneDrive syncing is active, making code changes appear invisible in browser
solution: Run `npm run dev:clean` before testing changes to force cache clear and proper component recompilation. OneDrive's file-locking can interfere with Next's file watcher.

problem: Tab contrast and visual hierarchy for card plan selection UI
solution: Invert selected tab with solid ink background + paper text (matching nav LIVE pill pattern), increase padding ~50% (12px/10px), scale plan letter 16→22px bold, objective tag 9.5px bold mono with wide letter-spacing, apply 3px bottom rule in teal when APPLIED state present

Implemented and split into reviewable local commits: - `a631f69` — `fix(api): isolate runway closure RNG` - `db8ce32` — `feat(api): persist and replay simulator scenarios` - `64ec771` — `feat(web): unify landing and simulator design` Verification: - Full API suite: **116 passed** - Persistence/reseed tests in both orde

problem: CI failures from recent commits: ruff lint/format violations and mypy type mismatch on duck-typed client parameter
solution: Run `ruff check --fix` and `ruff format` locally to catch import/formatting issues before push. For duck-typed parameters that pass mypy structurally, use `cast()` explicitly when type checker can't infer compatibility (e.g., `_FrozenWeatherClient` → `WeatherClient` in `replay.py:83`). Reproduce full CI pipeline locally when `gh` CLI unavailable: ruff check, ruff format, mypy, pytest, tsc, npm build.

problem: WebSocket state transitions in separate async processes (voice_daemon StateHub and agent-core WsHub) can emit out-of-order when same-millisecond state changes are queued as independent async tasks
solution: Serialize all StateHub broadcasts through a single ordered queue instead of calling call_soon_threadsafe + create_task per frame — ensures client sees deterministic state sequence (e.g., thinking→speaking→idle, not thinking→idle→speaking)

problem: Network audit intercepts http.request but misses fetch calls—SDK and connectors use globalThis.fetch (undici), never node:http, so audit reports false 'zero external calls'
solution: Patch globalThis.fetch in NetworkMonitor alongside http/https.request; restore both in stop(); add real fetch test (https://example.com) instead of simulated no-op to validate interception works.

problem: Completed missions trapped in UI with no escape controls when delivery pipeline blocked by failed tests or dirty repo
solution: Render Archive and Continue controls unconditionally on completed missions, not just when delivery succeeds. Archive button provides escape hatch when commit/push are permanently gated. Do not add 'force commit' bypass — that guard prevents mixing pre-existing work into mission commits intentionally.

problem: Running server continues serving stale code after backend changes until explicitly restarted with original command
solution: After backend updates, kill the existing server process and restart with its original command (e.g. `python dashboard/serve.py 8817`). Verify new code is live by probing a new endpoint or checking for new error signatures that only exist in updated code.

problem: GitHub repository rename breaks remote URL; future fetches/pushes silently fall back to redirect
solution: After repo rename, update local remote: `git remote set-url origin <new-url>`. Verify with `git remote -v`. This prevents reliance on GitHub's temporary redirect and ensures fast-forward pushes work cleanly.
